title: How LLM agents rewired my entire coding workflow
author: Code Coup
text:
Member-only story
How LLM agents rewired my entire coding workflow
LLM coding agents changed how I write software. Less typing, more thinking.
I didn’t notice it happening at first.
That’s the scary part.
One week, I was still typing most of my code, fighting with edge cases, leaning on autocomplete like a crutch.
A few weeks later I was mostly… talking. To a model. In English.
And the code just appeared.
Not toy code. Real code. The kind you ship and then have to maintain when you’re tired, annoyed, and slightly resentful of your past self.
I have been programming for seven years. I’ve lived through frameworks, rewrites, microservices, monorepos, and the endless parade of “this will change everything.”
Nothing moved this fast.
Nothing changed my muscle memory this deeply.
So these are just notes. From the last few weeks of coding, almost entirely with agents.
Somewhere between November and December, my workflow flipped.
It went from 80% manual coding + autocomplete, 20% agents
to 80% agents, 20% edits and touchups.
Which means something slightly embarrassing to admit out loud:
